title: Thelypteris costulisora
intro: Thelypteris costulisora, also known as the costulate glade fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce pain and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris costulisora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Thelypteris costulisora is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Thelypteris costulisora is classified under the following ranks: Kingdom - Plantae, Phylum - Pteridophyta, Class - Polypodiopsida, Order - Polypodiales, Family - Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Thelypteris and the species costulisora. The species further falls into the subfamily Thelypteridoideae and the tribe Thelypterideae. This classification is based on various sources including botanical studies and taxonomic research. Further classification may also include infraspecific ranks such as subspecies or varieties, but these are not well established for Thelypteris costulisora.
